description:
Implement Data Transparency Feature for AI Report Generation

Add functionality that allows users to view and verify the source data used to generate AI reports, ensuring transparency about data freshness and completeness.

Requirements:
- Add a "View Source Data" button or link on the report page at https://agritech-dashboard.thebzlab.online/parcels/628c528e-0914-4b9c-b182-4411eac3b857/reports
- Display metadata about the data used including:
  * Date range of data included in the report
  * Timestamp of when data was last updated
  * Number of data points or records used
  * Any data sources that were included or excluded
- Create a modal or expandable section showing the actual raw/processed data used for report generation
- Add visual indicators for data freshness (e.g., green for recent, yellow for aging, red for stale)
- Define and display thresholds for what constitutes "enough data" vs "insufficient data"
- Show warnings or alerts if data is stale or insufficient before report generation
- Allow users to refresh/update data before regenerating the report if needed
- Ensure data view is read-only to prevent accidental modifications
- Log data timestamps with each report for audit trail purposes

Edge Cases to Consider:
- Handle scenarios where some data sources are available but others are missing
- Account for partial data updates across different data sources
- Display appropriate messaging when no data is available for the selected time period
- Handle large datasets with pagination or sampling in the data view

Success Criteria:
- Users can confidently verify the recency and completeness of data before trusting report insights
- Clear visibility into data staleness reduces uncertainty about report accuracy
- Data transparency builds user trust in AI-generated recommendations
